snippet.title: MemPalace - How Milla Jovovich's AI Project Scammed the Internet
snippet.description:
MemPalace, the celebrity-backed AI memory system launched by Milla Jovovich, promised revolutionary performance and open-source freedom. It quickly went viral, attracting thousands of stars on GitHub and spawning a memecoin. But behind the polished marketing and impossible claims, independent researchers uncovered a shocking truth: MemPalace's benchmarks were rigged, its features fabricated, and its code a mess. This is the story of how A-I hype and celebrity endorsement created a mirage that fooled the internet.


 Sources:                                                                      
  - MemPalace repo: https://github.com/milla-jovovich/mempalace                
  - Benchmark methodology issues:                                               
  https://github.com/milla-jovovich/mempalace/issues/29                         
  - Independent code analysis:                                                  
  https://github.com/lhl/agentic-memory/blob/main/ANALYSIS-mempalace.md         
  - BENCHMARKS.md (their own admissions):                     
  https://github.com/milla-jovovich/mempalace/blob/main/benchmarks/BENCHMARKS.md

snippet.title: The best arrangement #movie #film
snippet.description:
πüé§ Visuals :The Devil Wears Prada 
It tells the story of Andy Sachs, an aspiring journalist who unexpectedly becomes the junior assistant to Miranda Priestly, the formidable editor-in-chief of Runway magazine and the reigning queen of the fashion world.

Initially, Andy is a complete misfit in the glamorous, image-obsessed world she enters. To survive and earn Miranda's approval, she undergoes a profound transformationβÄîinside and outβÄîwith the help of her sharp-witted colleague, Nigel. She becomes highly efficient and stylish. However, as she grows increasingly adept at this "job a million girls would kill for," her personal life and moral compass begin to unravel.

The film's central conflict lies in Andy's awakening. She witnesses the cold calculation and personal sacrifices behind the industry's glittering facade, ultimately realizing that the price of success may be too high. In an iconic moment, she chooses to walk away, tossing her phone into a fountain and voluntarily relinquishing the coveted position.

This is not merely a tale of a "devilish boss," but a coming-of-age parable about choice and self-definition. Andy departs with the skills and wisdom gained from the experience, but without being assimilated by it. The film concludes with Miranda offering Andy a faint, approving smileβÄîa signal that Andy has earned genuine respect. She found herself precisely by refusing to be changed.

snippet.title: How I've Been Using the reMarkable 2 (2023) for 3 Months
snippet.description:
Get reMarkable 2: https://bit.ly/3oppbRD

I've spent 3 months with reMarkable 2 - a paper tablet that is designed to improve your focus. reMarkable 2 was actually released a few years ago, but it's been updating the software a lot since then, and now it feels like a very different tablet. Many people have difficulties choosing between a tablet like reMarkable or an all-in-one tablet like iPad. While iPad can do anything, it does come with distractions. You may not be able to watch movies or play games on reMarkable, but its ability to create an environment that allows you to concentrate for a long period of time is what makes it worth it.

snippet.title: The Most Insane Ski Run Ever Imagined - Markus Eder's The Ultimate Run
snippet.description:
Watch the exclusive behind the scenes on Red Bull TV πüëâ https://www.redbull.com/int-en/episodes/the-ultimate-run-s1-e2

βÄ€Sometimes my mind goes kind of crazy about skiing and I ask myself, what if...?βÄù - Markus Eder

What if you could link every powder turn, every rail, every cliff drop, every comp run and every kicker nailed into one ultimate run? Well, Markus Eder did just that in βÄ‰The Ultimate RunβÄô!

This is MarkusβÄô Opus Magnum, a medley of face shots, massive tricks and even bigger drops, which was documented by Innsbruck based production company Legs of Steel over the past two years.

Markus has been visualizing the ultimate run since 2015. It may look like a simple undertaking in the final edit, but for arguably the most versatile skier on the planet, it meant taking his skill levels in every form and style of contemporary freeskiing to the next level.

βÄ€All aspects of freeskiing have fascinated me since the beginningβÄù, states Markus.

The Ultimate Run kicks off on the lofty extremities of Zermatt as Markus drops into a sheer expanse of powder, before shredding his way through glacial blocks the size of buses, jumping off ice cliffs and then slips into the belly of the glacier, only to reappear above his home resort of Klausberg. Carving fields of fresh powder he joins a session with his buddies at his local snowpark, before boosting back off into the backcountry for some more face shots. The firecrackers keep on popping as he enters the snow covered architecture of Taufer castle and a mining museum. As the sun sets over the mountains Markus slides out onto the valley floor, six years of dream skiing condensed into ten minutes of pure joy and adrenaline. 

This edit took over 90 days to film, entirely in the Alps in MarkusβÄô home region. βÄ€The Ultimate Run is MarkusβÄô dream project,βÄù says Tobi Reindl from Legs of Steel, βÄ€and it also became one of the biggest and most thrilling projects we have done so far.βÄù

With the first snow falling in the Alps, βÄ‰The Ultimate RunβÄô is a must watch for all winter sports enthusiasts. It will reignite the passion for winter in even the most laid back snow fans for the coming season.

Click play in the video above and find the answer to the question, βÄ€what if?βÄù...

snippet.description:
How to Cook Steak Perfectly Every Time | The Stay At Home Chef 
__________¬≠βÜ™βÜ™βÜ™βÜ™βÜ™βÜ™ CLICK FOR RECIPE βÜ™βÜ™βÜ™βÜ™βÜ™βÜ™βÜ™βÜ™ ______________
Learn how to cook steak perfectly every single time with this easy to follow recipe where steak is seared in a skillet on the stove and finished in the oven. 





INGREDIENTS

2 beef steaks, at least 1-inch thick
2 teaspoons olive oil
1 teaspoon salt
1/2 teaspoon black pepper
1/2 teaspoon white pepper
2 tablespoons softened butter
1-2 cloves garlic, minced



INSTRUCTIONS

1. Remove steaks from refrigerator and any packaging and let sit at room temperature for at least 30 minutes.

2. Rub each side with the olive oil to coat. Combine the salt, pepper, and onion powder in a small bowl. Rub both sides of each steak with the rub mixture.

3. Preheat oven to 375 degrees. Preheat a heavy, oven-safe skillet over high heat until it is smoking hot. Sear steaks in the hot pan for 2-3 minutes per side. If the steak has a side of fat, turn the steak onto its side and render the fat by searing it for 2-3 minutes as well.

4. Slide the skillet with the seared steaks in it into the oven to finish cooking. Remove the steaks from the oven 5 degrees before the desired level of doneness is achieved, or 130 degrees F for medium rare.

5. While the steaks are cooking, use a fork to combine the butter with garlic in a small bowl. 

6. When the steaks are removed from the grill, immediately top with 1 tablespoon of the butter mixture and allow the steaks to rest 10 minutes before cutting into them. The temperature of the steak will continue to rise as it rests by about 5 degrees.

snippet.title: Scrum vs Kanban: Two Agile Teams Go Head-to-Head
snippet.description:
This is the tale of two Agile teams. It wasn't just an organisational separation: it was an AGILE separation. 

= = = = = = = = = = = = 

New for 2024: my best-ever training:
"How Your Agile Teams Can Achieve Predictability and Productivity WITHOUT Burnout"
βÜ£ https://www.developmentthatpays.com/webinars/p-and-p?ref=yt

= = = = = = = = = = = = 

Download your FREE CHEAT SHEET: https://www.developmentthatpays.com/cheatsheets/scrum-kanban

This is a story of Two Agile Teams. 

More correctly, itβÄôs the tale of one Agile Team that split into two Agile Teams.

What makes the story interesting is that it was more than just an organisational separation. It was an Agile separation:

- One team continued as before - with *Scrum*
- The other team dropped Scrum in favour of *Kanban*

Will it all end in tears?




-------------------
100. Scrum vs Kanban: Two Agile Teams Go Head-to-Head
 #ScrumVsKanban #AgileScrum #Kanban #DevelopmentThatPays

This is a Tale of Two Agile Teams. More correctly, itβÄôs the tale of one Agile Team that split into two Agile Teams. It was more than an organisational separation. It was an Agile separation. Act One   It was 2012 when I first walked into the offices of the BBC. The same time and the building as these guysβÄΠ Never saw them. Which is strange. The software team I joined was one, I was told, that 'did Agile'. At the time, I knew very little about 'Agile'. But I could see from the get go that they werenβÄôt doing it by halves. ThereβÄôd clearly been lots of training. They had all kinds of tools. They were doing all kinds of 'rituals'. WeβÄôll get into the specifics of how the team worked in a minute. The Split ----- Fast forward a year and the department reorganised. My team was split in two. Although reporting lines changed, the seating plan didnβÄôt. There was one outward indication of change: where there had been one agile board, there were now two. Oh, and we did two stand-ups every day: ours at 10:00am, theirs at 10:15. A New Flavour ------- Ever-observant, it took me a couple of weeks to notice that the other team was doing a different 'flavour' of Agile. I hadnβÄôt realised that there was more than one flavour of Agile! What my team was doing was, called Scrum.    The other team was doing something called Kanban.  Kanban  Really This was a word I knew from way back. But I knew it in the context of manufacturing. I couldnβÄôt immediately see how it applied to the process used by my (former) teammates. So I went to talk to the Lead Developer of 'Team Kanban'. 'What the difference between Scrum and Kanban ' I asked  He was ready with an immediate answer: 'You Guys Talk About Work. We Do Work.' Ouch! in that moment, I learned an important lesson about Agile: it can be an emotive issue. Beliefs can be deep-seated. The Team Kanban Lead Dev clearly thought that Kanban was better than Scrum. I heldβÄΠ the opposite view. My view was both strongly heldβÄΠ and completely without evidential foundation. Natural Experiment ---- IβÄôm a little older now. And, I hope, a little wiser. I can now see that the team split was a perfect Natural Experiment. You know the kind of thing: βÄ€Take two identical twins. Separate them at birth. Feed one Scrum. Feed the other Kanban. Observe the result.βÄù So I hope youβÄôll join me on a little forensic investigation, starting with a 20,000 view of each team's processes. Team Scrum ----- My team - letβÄôs call it "Team Scrum" - worked in two-week Sprints. At the beginning of s Sprint, weβÄôd take ourselves off to a quiet part of the building for a Sprint Planning session. The Product Owner would select items from the backlog, and weβÄôd play βÄ€Planning PokerβÄù to estimate the size each item. WeβÄôd continue until we had roughly one βÄ€SprintβÄôs worthβÄù of cards. Sprint Planning over, each developer would pick up a card and set to work Every morning thereβÄôd be a Stand-up - aka a Daily Scrum - 10 am on the dot. And so it would go on day after day, with the cards gradually making their way across the board. By the about the Tuesday of the second week, weβÄôd expect all of the cards to have moved at least one step. It was then a race - a "sprint" - to get everything tested and ready for release on Friday. We didnβÄôt always succeed in getting everything across the board: any item that failed to make it would be βÄ€recycledβÄù into the next Sprint. On the Friday morning, everything in the release column would be packaged for release. Oh, and one last thing to round out the Sprint: a Retrospective: a chance for the team to get together to reflect on what well, to discuss what could be improved, and to commit to one or two action items for the following Sprint.
